<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> - Bye.<|1.32|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> that true identity as children of one God,<|4.92|> <|5.82|> and the call to each of us to be healers and peacemakers.<|10.68|> <|14.38|> In August, as some of you may have read in our cathedral's newsletter,<|19.42|> <|20.34|> we're going to be a church.<|20.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I served with Jerusalem Peace Builders, an interfaith nonprofit that brings together<|7.24|> <|7.24|> Americans, Israelis, and Palestinians for peace and leadership programs.<|13.12|> <|13.12|> And this past summer was my 10th year running these camps for teens.<|18.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.92|> Our approach is simple.<|2.54|> <|3.64|> To imagine a future, a new future in the Holy Lands, you start with the youth, the next generation.<|11.42|> <|12.98|> Through workshops and dialogues, service projects and visits to Muslim, Jewish and Christian faith communities,<|20.46|> <|21.32|> participants are offered a unique opportunity.<|24.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> opportunity to transform themselves and each other, to break free from the competing narratives<|10.46|> <|10.46|> of their societies, to redirect their lives and live not as adversaries and enemies, but<|20.20|> <|20.20|> as neighbors and friends.<|23.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.28|> This humble service is both incredible and heartbreaking at the same time,<|6.80|> <|7.64|> for our work has been dramatically set back by recent events.<|13.04|> <|14.36|> However, this life that is revealed in JPB,<|19.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> in Jerusalem, peace builders, is a bold reflection of the one read about in that scroll in that<|10.58|> <|10.58|> synagogue, the one proclaimed on the Mount of the Beatitudes, the one proclaimed on<|17.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.22|> It is a life that we do not just give up on and walk away, but it is one that requires continual recommitment, prayer and faithfulness, nourishment and community and sacrament.<|20.20|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.82|> For through death, there is life.<|6.44|> <|9.28|> What I've learned that I want to close and offer to you in this work is that peacemaking is not just reserved for places like the Middle East, but it is called for and commanded right here in the world.<|26.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> the reconciliation of our conflicts,<|4.10|> <|5.32|> the renewal and redemption and healing of our souls.<|9.80|> <|11.86|> On this night as we are together,<|14.18|> <|15.38|> I ask how might I be called,<|19.32|> <|19.78|> I ask you how might you be called to be a healer?<|24.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> How might you <|9.28|> What I've learned that I want to close and offer to you in this work is that peacemaking is not just reserved for places like the Middle East, but it is called for and commanded right here in the world.<|26.30|>
